cards.q: What is auditing in the context of SQL Server?
cards.a: Tracking events and changes that occur in the Database Engine. (الصفحة 3)

cards.q: Which component allows users to track audit events in SQL Server?
cards.a: SQL Server Audit. (الصفحة 3)

cards.q: What is an Audit object?
cards.a: A collection of server actions or database actions (these actions might also be grouped together). (الصفحة 3)

cards.q: To keep track of events (called actions), what must be defined when creating an audit?
cards.a: A collection, or audit. The actions you want to track are collected according to an audit specification. Recording those actions is done by the target (destination). (الصفحة 3)

cards.q: What does auditing give users the ability to do regarding recorded events?
cards.a: Track and record those actions at both the instance and database level. (الصفحة 3)

cards.q: What is Auditing in the context of SQL Server and Azure SQL Database?
cards.a: It is the act of tracking and recording events that occur in the Database Engine. (Page 6)

cards.q: How does SQL Server Audit track actions at both the instance and database level?
cards.a: It uses extended events to give you the ability to track and record those actions. (Page 6)

cards.q: What are the three main components needed to track actions (events)?
cards.a: A collection (audit), an audit specification, and a target (destination). (Page 6)

cards.q: Where are audits logged, and why is the audit target written asynchronously?
cards.a: Audits are logged to event logs or audit files; they are written asynchronously for performance reasons. (Page 6)

cards.q: What does the SQL Server `audit` object represent?
cards.a: A collection of server actions or database actions. (Page 6)

cards.q: What does a Server audit object define?
cards.a: The actions to collect at the instance level or database level (for all databases on the instance). (الصفحة 8)

cards.q: What must not be monitored in a database audit specification?
cards.a: Server-scoped objects. (الصفحة 8)

cards.q: Name three possible destinations for sending audit results.
cards.a: The Windows Security event log, the Windows Application event log, or an audit file on the file system. (الصفحة 8)

cards.q: How many database audits can be created per database?
cards.a: Only one database audit can be created per database per audit. (الصفحة 8)

cards.q: What two types of groups can you monitor using audit events?
cards.a: Audit events and audit action groups. (الصفحة 8)

cards.q: To begin creating a server audit in SQL Server Management Studio, where should you look?
cards.a: In Object Explorer, expand the Security folder. (الصفحة 10)

cards.q: After expanding the Security folder, what action is taken to initiate the creation of an audit?
cards.a: Right-click the Audits folder and select a shortcut. (الصفحة 10)

cards.q: If 'File' is selected in the Audit Destination list box during audit configuration, what must be entered?
cards.a: A valid file path. (الصفحة 12)

cards.q: What is recommended when entering a name into the Audit Name box?
cards.a: Choosing an appropriate name, as the default name is based on the current date and time. (الصفحة 12)

cards.q: Under what condition can an audit specification be created?
cards.a: Only if an audit already exists. (الصفحة 10)

cards.q: In Object Explorer, where do you find the option to create a server audit specification?
cards.a: Expand the Security folder, right-click the Server Audit Specification folder, and click New Server Audit Specification (الصفحة 14)

cards.q: When creating a new server audit specification, what must be selected in the Audit list box?
cards.a: The previously created server audit (الصفحة 14)

cards.q: After creating an audit, what action is necessary to make sure it functions?
cards.a: Right-click the newly created audit and click Enable Audit (الصفحة 14)

cards.q: Besides naming, what can be chosen when setting up a server audit specification?
cards.a: One or more audit actions, or audit action groups (الصفحة 14)

cards.q: What is the first step to begin creating a server audit specification in SQL Server Management Studio?
cards.a: In Object Explorer, expand the Security folder (الصفحة 14)

cards.q: Where is the location of the database audit specification?
cards.a: Under the database security context (Page 16)

cards.q: In Object Explorer, which folder must be expanded to find the Database Audit Specifications?
cards.a: The Security folder (Page 16)

cards.q: What are two ways you can view audit logs?
cards.a: SQL Server Management Studio or the Security Log in the Windows Event Viewer (Page 16)

cards.q: What minimum permission level is required to view or modify the Audit Log?
cards.a: Control Server (Page 16)

cards.q: When creating a database audit specification, what must be remembered regarding its activation?
cards.a: To use the context menu to turn it on (Page 16)
